intro.uz: Taoizm — tabiat bilan kurashmay, oqim bilan yashash falsafasi (wu wei — majburlamaslik). Alan Wattsning fikricha, ong osti miya eng kuchli qism: yechimni zoʻrlab emas, unga imkon berib topamiz. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi ham shu gʻoyaga mos: ehtimollik modallari (might / may / could — majburlamaslik) va majhul nisbat 1 (is done / was done — bajaruvchisiz ish).
intro.en: Taoism — the philosophy of living in harmony with nature instead of fighting it (wu wei — non-forcing). Alan Watts argues that the subconscious mind is the most powerful part: we find answers by allowing, not forcing. This week's two grammar topics fit that idea: modals of possibility (might / may / could) and the agentless passive (is done / was done).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida noaniq rejalar va ehtimolliklarni might / may / could bilan toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz va oddiy ishlarni majhul nisbatda (is done / was done) — bajaruvchini koʻrsatmasdan — tasvirlab bera olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can talk about uncertain plans and possibilities with might / may / could without pausing, and describe simple actions in the passive (is done / was done) without naming the doer.

grammar.titleEn: Modals of possibility — might / may / could
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — hedge predictions and future plans naturally (it might…, it may…)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can express degrees of possibility about the present and future
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada ehtimol maʼnosi feʼl oxirida yashiringan: “yogʻ-ishi mumkin”, “bils-a kerak”, yoki alohida “balki/ehtimol” soʻzi bilan. Ingliz tilida esa ehtimol feʼldan OLDIN alohida modal soʻz bilan chiqadi (might/may/could) va undan keyin feʼl sof shaklda qoladi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ It might to rain. She may knows. ✅ It might rain. She may know. Modaldan keyin feʼl doim sof (base) shaklda: “to” ham, “-s” ham qoʻshilmaydi, modalning oʻzi ham oʻzgarmaydi.

examples.en: The answer might come by itself.
You may be disappointed if you use brute strength.
examples.uz: Qoʻpol kuch ishlatsangiz, hafsalangiz pir boʻlishi mumkin.

examples.en: You could just let it flow.
examples.uz: Shunchaki oqimga qoʻyib berishingiz ham mumkin.

exercises.prompt: It ___ rain later, so take an umbrella.
exercises.hintUz: modal + sof feʼl: might rain (“to” va “-s” yoʻq)

exercises.prompt: Don't strain — the solution ___ come by itself.
She ___ know the answer; let's ask her calmly.
exercises.hintUz: modal oʻzgarmaydi va keyin sof feʼl keladi: could know

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda ehtimolli uchta reja ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “Tonight I might … Tomorrow I may … This weekend I could … .”

grammar.titleUz: Majhul nisbat 1: is done / was done (bajaruvchisiz)
grammar.titleEn: Passive voice 1 — is done / was done
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Writing Task 1 & 2 — describe processes and facts impersonally (is made, was built)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can form simple present and past passives when the doer is unknown or unimportant
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada majhul nisbat feʼlning ICHIDA -il-/-in- qoʻshimchasi bilan yasaladi va bir soʻz yetadi: “eshik yopildi”, “ish qilindi”. Ingliz tilida esa doim IKKI qism kerak: be feʼli + feʼlning 3-shakli (was closed, is done). Oʻzbek oʻquvchilar aynan “be” feʼlini tushirib qoldiradi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ The door closed by me. The book wrote in English. ✅ The door was closed by me. The book was written in English. Majhulda “be” (am/is/are/was/were) hech qachon tushmaydi, asosiy feʼl esa V3 (3-shakl) boʻladi.

examples.en: English is taught with stories here.
examples.uz: Bu yerda ingliz tili hikoyalar bilan oʻrgatiladi.

examples.en: The book was written by Alan Watts.
We are forced to ignore almost everything.
examples.uz: Biz deyarli hamma narsani eʼtiborsiz qoldirishga majbur boʻlamiz.

exercises.prompt: The book ___ written by Alan Watts.
The door was ___ by me this morning.

exercises.promptUz: Uyingizdagi ikki ishni majhul nisbatda ayting: “… is done every day. … was made … .”
exercises.hintUz: be + V3 (is done / was made); “be” ni tushirmang

vocab.defEn: to try too hard, using too much effort
vocab.example: Don't strain your mind; contemplate the problem calmly.

vocab.defEn: to think about something calmly and slowly
vocab.example: Contemplate your goal instead of forcing an answer.

vocab.defEn: to imagine or picture something in your mind
vocab.example: Visualize your question, then simply wait.

vocab.uz: hayvoniy, qoʻpol kuch (juda kuchli, ammo qoʻpol)
vocab.defEn: raw, animal-like power; the opposite of subtle
to watch and control something carefully
vocab.example: You don't need to supervise your heartbeat.

vocab.defEn: one line or way; one thing at a time
vocab.example: Conscious thinking runs along a single track.

vocab.defEn: to think about something before deciding
vocab.example: She took the baby gorilla into consideration.
The subconscious handles innumerable things at once.

vocab.defEn: to manage or deal with something
vocab.example: Your brain can handle many variables at the same time.
Words are crude; they work one by one.

quiz.q: How many times does a mouse sigh in an hour, on average?
quiz.qUz: Sichqon soatiga oʻrtacha necha marta xoʻrsinadi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 40 marta. Catherine 40 dedi va haq boʻldi. Sichqonlarning yuragi tezroq uradi, shuning uchun oʻpkasini koʻproq “qayta sozlashi” (reset) kerak — inson esa oʻrtacha soatiga 12 marta xoʻrsinadi.

vocab.uz: omon qolish mexanizmi (tirik qolish uchun beixtiyor qilinadigan ish)
vocab.defEn: something the body does automatically in order to survive

vocab.uz: qayta ishga tushirmoq, dastlabki holatga qaytarmoq
vocab.defEn: to return something to its original settings
a physical action or reaction you can't control

vocab.en: to breathe a sigh of relief
vocab.defEn: to feel relief when something bad has not happened
the feeling you have when things aren't going well
accepting something bad that you cannot change

funEnglish.title: Alan Watts — The Watercourse Way: Wu Wei & Effortless Action

speakingPrompt.en: Think about a problem you are trying to solve. What might happen if you stop forcing it? Describe how the answer could come by itself — and say what is usually done, step by step, when the problem is finally solved.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: ehtimollik modallari (it might…, it may…, you could…) va majhul nisbat (is done / was done — bajaruvchini koʻrsatmasdan).
